messages.content: Ground effect may make a small multirotor shift sideways easily when approaching the ground. True or False?

True. Ground effect refers to the increased lift and decreased drag experienced by an aircraft (including multirotors) when flying very close to the ground. This can cause disturbances in the airflow around the aircraft, leading to changes in its stability and control. One common effect of ground effect is the tendency for small multirotors to shift sideways easily when approaching the ground.

What is the minimum visibility for visual navigation by air??

10km or more
1km
400 feet
5km / 5000m

The minimum visibility for visual navigation by air is typically 5 kilometers or 5000 meters. This distance allows pilots to have clear visibility of the surrounding airspace and navigational landmarks for safe and efficient flight.

You are wanting to fly near a non-controlled aerodrome, and you need to find the contact number to discuss with a representative at that specific airport. Where do you find this?

NOTAMs
ERSA
AIP
Google

The correct answer is in the AIP (Aeronautical Information Publication). The AIP contains comprehensive information on all aspects of aviation including air navigation, airport facilities, procedures, and regulations. It provides contact information for airports, including non-controlled aerodromes, allowing pilots to find the relevant phone number to discuss with a representative at a specific airport.

You notice as you fly higher your indicated airspeed is reducing even though your ground speed is remaining the same. What is happening and what could you do to increase your performance?

Climb higher as you are in wind shear
Descend to a lower altitude as you are being affected by wind gradient
Fly straight and level as it will get better over time
Turn around as it’s not safe to keep flying in this direction

The most likely scenario in this case is that you are experiencing wind shear. Wind shear refers to a sudden change in wind speed and/or direction at different altitudes. In this situation, the indicated airspeed decreases because the actual airspeed remains constant, but you are flying into a headwind component at higher altitudes.

To increase your performance in this scenario, you could descend to a lower altitude where the wind shear may be less pronounced. This would allow you to regain your indicated airspeed and potentially improve your ground speed as well. It is important to monitor weather conditions and make appropriate adjustments to your flight altitude to ensure safe and efficient performance.

Redundancy is an important factor on any flying system. Which of the following are common to RPAs requiring permissions such as flying over crowds?

GPS sensor, IMU, Propellers
GPS sensor, IMU, Data link communication, Cameras
GPS sensor, IMU, Data link communication, Power module, six or more motors
All the above

All the above options are common to RPAs (Remotely Piloted Aircraft) requiring permissions such as flying over crowds. Redundancy is crucial to ensure the safety and reliability of the system. Common components that are redundant in such RPAs typically include the GPS sensor (for accurate positioning and navigation), IMU (Inertial Measurement Unit for tracking and orientation), Data link communication (for reliable communication between the ground control station and the aircraft), Cameras (for monitoring and capturing visuals), Power module (for power supply redundancy), and six or more motors (for multirotor redundancy in case of motor failure). The combination of these redundant systems helps maintain the stability, control, and safety of the RPA during flight operations.

Internal resistance is the best way to measure battery?

Deterioration over time
Peak power usage
Charge capacity
Charge voltage

Internal resistance is not the best way to measure a battery's charge capacity, charge voltage, or peak power usage. Internal resistance refers to the opposition to the flow of current within the battery itself. It is primarily used to assess the health and deterioration of a battery over time.

To measure a battery's charge capacity, one would typically use methods such as controlled charging and discharging cycles or monitoring the voltage and current during charging and discharging processes. The charge voltage can be measured using a voltmeter, while peak power usage is typically determined by monitoring the power draw during periods of high energy demand.

While internal resistance is an important characteristic to consider, it is not the primary method for measuring battery capacity, voltage, or peak power usage.

When operating the RPA in controlled airspace, you have experienced a motor failure. The best course of action

Contact and advise ATC, Trouble shoot check and try to restart , navigate the RPA for forced landing if possible 
(Communicate, Aviate, Navigate)
Trouble shoot check and try to restart, navigate the RPA for forced landing if possible, contact and advise ATC
(Aviate, Navigate, Communicate)

The correct course of action when experiencing a motor failure while operating an RPA (Remotely Piloted Aircraft) in controlled airspace is to prioritize safety and follow the Aviate, Navigate, Communicate principle.

Aviate: The first step is to maintain control of the RPA and ensure its flight stability. In the case of a motor failure, the pilot should immediately troubleshoot the issue and attempt to restart the motor if possible while ensuring the safety of the aircraft.

Navigate: Once the RPA is under control, the pilot should assess the situation and determine the best course of action. If it is feasible and safe, the pilot should navigate the RPA to a suitable landing area considering terrain, obstacles, and clearance from people or property.

Communicate: After addressing the aviating and navigating aspects, the pilot should contact and advise the Air Traffic Control (ATC) of the situation. This allows ATC to be aware of the incident and potentially assist in coordinating the landing or providing any necessary instructions.

Therefore, the correct course of action is: Trouble shoot check and try to restart, navigate the RPA for forced landing if possible, and then contact and advise ATC (Aviate, Navigate, Communicate).

In a helicopter based RPA, in the event of motor failure inflight, what would be the course of action

Override from automation, perform autorotation, land into the wind
Raise the nose to slow down and land
Return to home
Perform 180 degrees turn and land

In the event of a motor failure in a helicopter-based RPA (Remotely Piloted Aircraft), the appropriate course of action would be to perform an autorotation and land into the wind.

Autorotation is a flight condition where the helicopter's rotor blades are allowed to spin freely without engine power. During an autorotation, the descent is controlled by manipulating the pitch of the rotor blades to maintain a safe descent rate.

To perform an autorotation, the pilot should override any automation if necessary and lower the collective to enter autorotation. They should also adjust the cyclic and pedals to control the descent and maintain control throughout the maneuver.

Landing into the wind is important because it helps in reducing the groundspeed, providing a safer and smoother landing. The pilot should aim for a suitable landing spot and execute the autorotation landing while maintaining control of the aircraft.

Therefore, the correct course of action in the event of motor failure in a helicopter-based RPA is to perform an autorotation and land into the wind.

In the event of inbound aircraft, the best course of action would be

Fly as fast as possible to avoid the aircraft
Descent as fast as possible but be aware of the vortex ring state, and land
Turn 180 degrees
Press Return to home

In the event of an inbound aircraft, the best course of action would be to descend as fast as possible but be aware of the vortex ring state and land.

Descending rapidly allows for increased separation between your aircraft and the inbound aircraft. However, it is important to be cautious of the vortex ring state, also known as settling with power, which is a dangerous condition that can occur during a rapid descent. It is characterized by a loss of effective rotor lift and a rapid descent rate.

Therefore, the recommended course of action would be to closely monitor the situation, initiate a descent as fast as possible while avoiding the vortex ring state, and then land safely. It is crucial to prioritize safety, maintain situational awareness, and avoid any abrupt maneuvers that could jeopardize the flight.

When equipping the RPA with emergency parachute, which of the following BEST needs to be considered

Maximum weight of the RPA
Maximum speed for safe deployment
Acceptable attitude of the RPA for safe deployment
All of the above

All of the above considerations need to be taken into account when equipping an RPA (Remotely Piloted Aircraft) with an emergency parachute.

1. Maximum Weight: The emergency parachute system needs to be suitable for the maximum weight of the RPA. It is important to ensure that the parachute can handle the weight of the entire aircraft, including any additional payloads or equipment.

2. Maximum Speed: The deployment of the parachute should be safe at the maximum speed of the RPA. The parachute system should be designed to handle the forces and dynamics associated with the RPA's top speed. It is crucial to prevent any issues or malfunctions that may arise from deploying the parachute at high speeds.

3. Acceptable Attitude: The RPA's attitude or orientation also plays a role in the safe deployment of the emergency parachute. Proper consideration should be given to ensure that the parachute can open and deploy correctly at different attitudes or angles of the RPA.

Taking all of these factors into consideration is essential to ensure the successful and safe deployment of the emergency parachute in an emergency situation.

How does a tri-copter achieve yaw control ?

by adjusting the rpm of the 2 front motor
by pivoting the rear motor
by using control surface
by rolling to one direction

A tri-copter achieves yaw control by adjusting the RPM (Rotations Per Minute) of the two front motors asymmetrically. By increasing the RPM of one front motor and decreasing the RPM of the other, the differential thrust causes the tri-copter to rotate around its vertical axis, resulting in yaw control.

Pivoting the rear motor may offer some yaw control, but it is not the primary method used in tri-copters. The control surface, typically used in fixed-wing aircraft, is not typically utilized for yaw control in tri-copters. Rolling to one direction also doesn't directly contribute to yaw control in a tri-copter.

What is the Datum of an aircraft?

Review Later
The very front
An imaginary line from which all measurements of arm are taken
The leading edge of the wing
The Centre of Gravity

The Datum of an aircraft is an imaginary reference line or point from which all measurements of arm (or moment) are taken. It provides a standard reference point for balance and weight calculations in aircraft design and operations. It is typically a fixed location or reference point established by the aircraft manufacturer and is often located at the front of the aircraft, but it may not necessarily coincide with the very front of the aircraft. The Datum is used to calculate the aircraft's center of gravity and ensure proper weight distribution for safe and efficient flight.

Class C airspace is representative of which of the below:

Review Later
Major Airports
Regional Airports
Medium altitude airspace
High altitude airspace

Class C airspace is representative of major airports. 

Class C airspace is typically designated around airports with a significant level of commercial and general aviation activity. It is designed to provide controlled and regulated airspace to ensure the safety and separation of aircraft operating in and around these airports. Class C airspace extends from the surface up to a specific altitude and is characterized by a circular boundary and various layers or tiers based on different altitudes. This type of airspace aims to accommodate the high volume of traffic associated with major airports.

What information is contained in the AIP?

Review Later
Weather and Terrain
Aeronautical Information Publication containing flight procedures
Prohibited, restricted and danger areas, including emergency procedures
B & C

The AIP (Aeronautical Information Publication) contains comprehensive aeronautical information that is essential for safe and efficient flight operations. This publication typically includes the following types of information:

1. Aeronautical Charts: The AIP contains a collection of charts and maps, including enroute charts, approach charts, airport diagrams, and other visuals that provide important navigational and procedural information.

2. Flight Procedures: The AIP provides detailed flight procedures for various types of operations, including takeoff, landing, approach, departure, and airway navigation. It outlines the necessary steps and requirements for safe and standardized aviation procedures.

3. Airspace Information: The AIP provides information on different types of airspace, such as controlled airspace, prohibited areas, restricted areas, and danger areas. It also includes specific details about clearance requirements, air traffic control procedures, and emergency procedures within these areas.

4. Airfield Information: The AIP offers information on airports, aerodromes, runways, taxiways, lighting systems, navigational aids, and other related infrastructure. It provides valuable data for pilots and operators when planning and executing flights.

5. Regulations and Rules: The AIP includes regulatory information, airspace rules, and relevant guidelines issued by the aviation authorities. It helps pilots and operators stay up to date with the current regulations and requirements.

These are just a few examples of the information contained in the AIP. It is an essential resource for pilots, navigators, and other aviation professionals to ensure safe and compliant flight operations.

You want to fly above 400ft but still in visual line of site, what do you need?

Review Later
NOTAM
Make radio calls to all inbound and outbound traffic
Area Approval
Area Approval and a NOTAM

To fly above 400 feet but within visual line of sight (VLOS), you generally need area approval and a NOTAM (Notice to Airmen).

Area Approval: This refers to obtaining authorization or permission from the appropriate aviation authorities or controlling entities to operate your aircraft in a specific area or airspace above 400 feet. The process may vary depending on the jurisdiction and regulations in place.

NOTAM: A NOTAM is a notice provided by aviation authorities to inform pilots about any temporary changes, hazards, or conditions that may affect flight operations. It includes information about airspace restrictions, temporary flight restrictions, or any other relevant information that pilots need to be aware of to ensure safe and orderly operations.

Making radio calls to inbound and outbound traffic is a good practice for situational awareness, but it may not be specifically required for flying above 400 feet within visual line of sight.

Therefore, to fly above 400 feet but within visual line of sight, you would generally need area approval and a NOTAM to ensure compliance with air traffic regulations and maximize safety.

At least how many hours' notice must CASA have for the lodgment of a NOTAM

Review Later
12hrs
24hrs
48hrs
72hrs

CASA (Civil Aviation Safety Authority) requires at least 12 hours' notice for the lodgement of a NOTAM (Notice to Airmen). This is the minimum advance notice required to provide information about temporary changes or potential hazards that may affect flight operations. Pilots and operators are responsible for submitting NOTAMs in advance to ensure that relevant information is disseminated to other aviation stakeholders in a timely manner.

If the pilot enters extreme turbulence, what should be done to minimize the stress on the aircraft.

Review Later
Increase speed to fly through it faster
Increase speed and climb above it
Reduce speed to manoeuvre through it
Reduce speed to the manufacturers documented manoeuvring speed of the aircraft

To minimize the stress on the aircraft when encountering extreme turbulence, it is generally recommended to reduce speed to the manufacturer's documented maneuvering speed for the specific aircraft.

Reducing the speed to the aircraft's maneuvering speed helps mitigate the forces and stresses on the airframe, making it more resilient to the turbulence. The maneuvering speed is the maximum speed at which full control inputs can be applied without risking structural damage to the aircraft. By flying at or below this speed, the pilot can maintain better control and reduce the likelihood of encountering structural issues or exceeding the aircraft's limitations.

Increasing speed to fly through turbulence faster or climbing above it may not be the most recommended course of action, as it may expose the aircraft to additional stresses or potential hazards associated with altitude changes.

Therefore, the best approach in the face of extreme turbulence is to reduce speed to the maneuvering speed recommended by the aircraft manufacturer.

What component would an electrical speed controller be used to control?

Review Later
Servos
Camera
Airspeed sensor
Brushless motor

An electrical speed controller (ESC) is specifically used to control a brushless motor in the context of remote-controlled (RC) aircraft or drones.

Brushless motors are commonly employed in RC aircraft and require an ESC to regulate their speed and direction. The ESC receives signals from the flight controller or receiver and adjusts the power supplied to the motor accordingly. By adjusting the pulse width modulation (PWM) signals, an ESC can control the rotational speed and response of the brushless motor.

Therefore, an electrical speed controller (ESC) is used to control and regulate the operation of a brushless motor.

From the power requirement formula, which component has the major effect on increasing the power needed to drive the system?
power = k x ( rpm^3 ) x (diameter^4 ) x (pitch)

From the given power requirement formula, it can be observed that the component with the major effect on increasing the power needed to drive the system is the diameter (d). 

In the formula, the diameter is raised to the power of 4, meaning that even a small increase in the diameter of the component can have a significant impact on the overall power requirement. This suggests that increasing the diameter of the component will result in a substantial increase in the power needed to drive the system.

Propeller size is expressed as a means of what dimensions

Review Later
Diameter x Thickness
Pitch x Hole diameter
Degrees of twist x RPM
Diameter x Pitch

Propeller size is expressed as a means of Diameter x Pitch.

The diameter of a propeller refers to the measurement of the circle formed by the rotating blades. It is typically measured in inches or millimeters.

The pitch of a propeller refers to the theoretical forward distance the propeller would travel with a single revolution assuming no slippage of air. It is commonly measured in inches or centimeters.

Therefore, when describing the size of a propeller, it is expressed as a combination of the diameter and pitch, such as "10x6" (10 inches diameter and 6 inches pitch) or "18x10" (18 inches diameter and 10 inches pitch).

Before charging any battery, what should be conducted first.

Review Later
Read manufacturers charging procedure and specifications
Plug in and charge straight away to save time
Wait until the outside temperature is below 25C
Read the chargers specification only.

Before charging any battery, it is crucial to first read the manufacturers charging procedure and specifications.

Different battery types and models have specific charging requirements and guidelines provided by the manufacturer. It is important to understand and follow these instructions to ensure safe and effective charging of the battery.

Simply plugging in and charging straight away without referring to the manufacturer's guidelines can lead to
